{"id":"project-LICHEN-den3","title":"announce: build_announce always increments seq_num before potential serialization failure","description":"File: python/src/lichen/announce/scheduler.py:161-187

The build_announce() method increments seq_num first, then performs operations that could fail:

```python
def build_announce(self) -> AnnounceMessage:
    seq = self._increment_seq()  # seq_num committed

    msg = AnnounceMessage(...)  # Could raise AnnounceError if identity invalid
    signature = sign(...)       # Could raise if crypto fails
    return AnnounceMessage(...)  # Second construction
```

If sign() or the AnnounceMessage constructor fails after seq_num was incremented:
1. Seq_num is 'burned' (skipped)
2. If persistence callback succeeded before failure, the persisted value is ahead
3. On retry, new seq_num is used

Impact: Seq_num gaps. While gaps are tolerated by seq_gt(), persistent failures could rapidly exhaust the 16-bit sequence space (65536 values).

Consider: Build and validate message BEFORE incrementing seq_num, then increment only when ready to transmit.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:20:19.43408Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:20:19.434109Z"}
